calls.note: Has given an uptick; almost all realty stocks have been consolidating for six months. With the RBI keeping interest rates steady, real estate can recover in Q2-Q4 depending on rates. Hold for the present (caller at a 30% loss asked about averaging).
Can be accumulated slowly from this range; momentum has been coming into banking and financial stocks for the last few days.
Same as HDFC Bank - accumulate slowly from this range on the back of the recent momentum in banking and financials.

Somewhat in a downtrend with medium-to-short-term pressure from rising GST, but it averages 15-17% CAGR in the long term. 'Three years is not long term - 5 to 10 years is.' For a 5-10 year investor there will be cycles in between, but it is a stock to hold on to.

Has corrected somewhat recently and the Reliance Jio listing is tomorrow. Large caps are underperforming; if foreign portfolio investors come back there is scope to outperform. For the long term - five to ten years - definitely hold.
Hold (mailer holding around 600). If averaging, do it only after this quarter's results. It is a buy-on-dips kind of stock for the long term.
Avoid in this range - after a big run-up the valuation looks stretched for this kind of business model. Only for momentum investing, and then take the position with a stop loss around the 450 range.
Both at about a 12% loss - hold for the present, but no averaging. Positions should be built up only after seeing the results.
Some of their molecules recently got USFDA approval. A good stock in the long term - if you have patience, slowly add on dips. Concord - buy.
